rationale: DIM1 (0): Treatment case; output gives misstatement_probability=45, which is <50, so per the treatment band rule (p>=70→2; 50<=p<70→1; p<50→0) it scores 0 despite the fraud being real. DIM2 (2): Top-ranked hypothesis targets SalesRevenueNet/AccountsReceivableNetCurrent with direction 'overstated' via 'premature or aggressive revenue recognition ... sales being booked ahead of cash collection.' Answer key (AAER-3845/33-10281) describes overstated distributor revenue via improper recognition on contingent/return-eligible sales — account area (revenue), direction (overstated), and treatment type (recognizing revenue before criteria met) substantially match → 2. It does NOT reach 3: no case-specific facts (distributor channel, contingent sales terms, implants shipped without instruments, discounts-as-expense) are named. Coverage note: ground truth is single-type (revenue_recognition); the output's secondary hypotheses (cash-flow timing, inventory valuation) are extraneous but not graded. DIM3 (2): Top hypothesis narrative (actively booking revenue prematurely, overstating revenue/AR) maps to 'active'; answer-key genre row is 'active(revenue)' — exact match → 2. DIM4 (3): Evidence cites concrete provided data points that genuinely support claims: AR 79.7M→90.2M (+13%) while revenue 282.2M→277.5M (-1.7%); NI vs OCF divergence in both FY2011 (-0.64M NI vs +38.9M OCF) and FY2012 (30.8M NI vs 6.7M OCF); inventory 53.3M→55.3M into Q1 2013 against -13.6% YoY Q1 revenue; goodwill inconsistency 107.9M vs 43.9M flagged with accession numbers. This is a coherent multi-year, multi-point combination → 3. Consistency rule check: p=45 with risk_tier='watch' satisfies 40<=p<70 => watch/elevated, so no cap. MEMORIZATION (false): Reasoning is anchored in concrete provided XBRL-style data values (not bare document listings), and no post-cutoff facts (SEC order, revelation, outcome) appear anywhere in the output.
